data.title: OPENING
blocks: The teacher reviewed Claude's output. The new About page rendered. The CSS looked right. The navigation included the new
Opening — Three days later, a student emailed: the link to the syllabus from the About page does not work.

S04.meta: // SLIDE 04 — STRONG VS. WEAK: THE WORKED TA

data.title: STRONG VS. WEAK: THE WORKED TABLE
blocks: Add a new page — "looks good"; "renders"
Change CSS — "no errors"; "looks better"
Deploy to server — "rsync exits 0"
data.section: STRONG VS. WEAK: THE WORKED TA

S05.meta: // SLIDE 05 — HOW TO WRITE ONE: FIVE QUESTIO

data.title: HOW TO WRITE ONE: FIVE QUESTIONS
blocks: · Claim of step? Add an About page; do not modify other files.
· Artifact? The new src/about.html and one nav-link change in src/index.html.
· Verification? Run npm run lint-html against the new file and against the rendered live site after deploy.
· Threshold? Lint exits 0; every link 200s on the deployment.
· Failure signature? If a link 404s, the cause is probably a relative path that resolved on local dev and not on the schoo
data.section: HOW TO WRITE ONE: FIVE QUESTIO

S06.meta: // SLIDE 06 — REVERT, DO NOT CORRECT FORWARD

data.title: REVERT, DO NOT CORRECT FORWARD: /REWIND
blocks: /rewind. Select the checkpoint before the failed step. Claude restores both the conversation context and the file change from both Claude's memory and your file system.

Respecify. Rewrite the original specification with the failure mode as an explicit negative constraint or as a new posit "All link paths in the new page must use absolute server-relative paths (leading /); never use page-relative paths that
· Re-run from clean state. Submit the revised specification. Claude executes against the corrected spec; the failure mode
· Re-verify. Run the handoff condition again. If it still fails, the specification is still wrong. /rewind again; tighten

data.title: SOME CONDITIONS REQUIRE HUMAN REVIEW.
blocks: Not every handoff condition can be a command. Some require human judgment — "the table is laid out in a visually balance
Some conditions require human review — The discipline for non-automatable conditions: name which human, at what point, performs the review. For your own builds

data.title: TEACHER BUILD: HANDOFF CONDITIONS FOR YOUR CLASS-WEBSITE PAGES
blocks: · Before approving any build step, evaluate the handoff condition. If automated (lint, test, command), run it. If visual,
· If the condition fails, /rewind to before the step. Respecify with the failure as a negative constraint. Re-run.
· If you fail twice on the same step, /clear and rewrite the specification from scratch with both failures as constraints.

data.title: CLASSROOM ACTIVITY: STUDENTS DESIGN HANDOFF CONDITIONS
blocks: · Each condition is specific, testable, binary.
· At least one condition uses an automated check (a command, a lint, a test).
· At least one condition includes a human-review pause for a property that cannot be automated.

data.title: COMMON MISCONCEPTIONS
blocks: "Looks good is a condition."
"Forward correction is fine."
"/rewind is for failures."
"Handoff conditions are for big builds."
"My grading scripts already work; I do not need handoff conditions."

data.title: AI WAYBACK MACHINE.
blocks: W. Edwards Deming
Plan-Do-Check-Act
quality is built into a process through explicit verification at every step, not inspected in at the end
andon-cord
